question: relative risk
answer:
(a) Crude analysis

$$
 egin{aligned}
& \hat{P}_{	ext {males }}=534 / 1198=0.4457 	ext { or about } 45 \% \
& \hat{P}_{	ext {errales }}=113 / 449=0.2517 	ext { or about } 25 \% \
& \widehat{R R}= rac{0.4457}{0.2517}=1.77
\end{aligned}
$$

(b) Applicants for major 1

$$
 egin{aligned}
& \hat{P}_{	ext {males }}=512 / 825=0.6206 	ext { about } 62 \% \
& \hat{P}_{	ext {females }}=89 / 108=0.8241 	ext { about } 82 \%
\end{aligned}
$$


Major 1 favors female applicants

(c) Applicants for major 2

$$
 egin{aligned}
& \hat{P}_{	ext {males }}=22 / 373=0.0590 	ext { or about } 6 \% \
& \hat{P}_{	ext {lemales }}=24 / 341=0.070 	ext { or about } 7 \%
\end{aligned}
$$


Major 2 favors female applicants.
(d) There is a slight bias for female applicants.
(e) The initial analysis was confounded because males tended to apply to major 1. Major 1 had a high acceptance rate ( 601 of $933=64 \%$ ), while major 2 had a low acceptance rate ( 46 of $714=6 \%$ ).
(f) Relative incidence of acceptance for males by major

$$
 egin{aligned}
& \widehat{R R}_1=0.753 \
& \widehat{R R}_2=0.838
\end{aligned}
$$


Test for interaction
A. Hypotheses. $H_0: R R_1=R R_2$ (no interaction) versus $H_2: R R_1 
eq R R_2$ (interaction)
B. Test statistic. $X^2{ }_{	ext {sat.int }}=0.136$ with $d f=1$
C. $P$-value. $P=0.713$ (no significant difference in strata-specific relative risk estimates; no significant interaction).
(g) $\widehat{R R}_{	ext {MH }}=0.77$. After adjusting for major, male applicants were $23 \%$ less likely to be accepted than female applicants.

meta info.caption: Sex bias in graduate school admissions?
